stories.headline: Iran war day 104: Iran says it attacks US bases after American strikes
stories.summary: Iran says it launched retaliatory strikes on US bases in the region after America launched new attacks inside Iran.

stories.headline: Iran war live: Tehran targets Bahrain, Kuwait, Jordan after new US strikes
stories.summary: Iran remains defiant amid US attacks, orders complete closure of Strait of Hormuz to all vessels.

stories.headline: US and Iran trade strikes for second day, threatening return of all-out war
stories.summary: The new US assault across multiple Iranian cities came as efforts to negotiate an end the war again appeared stuck, with Iran insisting it would maintain its chokehold on the Strait of Hormuz, which has disrupted global energy supplies and sent oil prices higher.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes across Middle East for second day in a row
stories.summary: Iran says it responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain, which reported damage to its capital city overnight.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange fresh wave of strikes
stories.summary: Oil price rises as Washington and Tehran send mixed signals over status of the Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: US Strikes Iran Again in Trump Escalation | The Asia Trade 6/11/2026
stories.summary:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.
 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Iran announces closure of Strait of Hormuz following US strikes
stories.summary: Iranian officials say the Strait of Hormuz has been closed in response to US strikes.

stories.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Haiti forced to change kit over war imagery
stories.summary: An illustration of the Battle of Vertieres in 1803, with the Haitian flag, had been embedded on the kits.

stories.headline: Which World Cup teams, players and officials were denied US visas, entry?
stories.summary: Top Somali referee Omar Artan latest victim of stringent US travel policies after Iranian squad's visas were delayed.

stories.headline: Why India’s deadly dengue crisis is now no longer confined to the monsoons
stories.summary: Experts warn that rising temperatures, erratic rainfall and rapid urbanisation have transformed the seasonal disease.

stories.headline: Funerals held after deadly Pakistani strikes in Afghanistan
stories.summary: Hundreds gathered in eastern Afghanistan for the funerals of civilians killed in Pakistani airstrikes.

stories.summary: Accountancy firms linked to the collapsed mortgage lender Market Financial Solutions Ltd. are being investigated by the UK’s audit watchdog.

Stickers, shirts and beer: 2026 World Cup could earn Portugal up to €945 million
stories.summary: Despite not hosting a single match, Portugal could see nearly €1 billion in economic benefits from a World Cup increasingly driven by digital engagement and consumer spending, according to a new study.

stories.headline: Thousands protest demanding Albania's PM Rama resigns
stories.summary: The demonstrations have grown into a broader anti-establishment movement, with protesters accusing both the government and opposition parties of failing to address the country's political and economic challenges.

stories.headline: Thousands march in Pakistan-administered Kashmir as clashes kill at least 15
stories.summary: An uneasy silence has blanketed the capital, with empty streets, closed shops and patrolling police.

stories.headline: China cancels high-level meetings with EU
stories.summary: Abrupt scrapping of two sets of planned talks in Beijing comes as tensions grow between trade superpowers

stories.headline: Indonesia Market Rout Resumes as Interest Rate Hike Bets Mount
stories.summary: Indonesia’s bonds and stocks resumed their selloff Thursday, underscoring expectations that the central bank will have to raise interest-rates again to limit capital outflows.

stories.headline: Kuwait Ships Cooking Gas Out of Hormuz as Gulf Producers Go Dark
stories.summary: Kuwait has shipped a cargo of liquefied petroleum gas out of the Persian Gulf through the Strait of Hormuz using a tanker it controls, as more producers opt for clandestine tactics to get energy to markets.

stories.headline: Indo Rate Hike Signals Resolve to Ease Angst: Robeco
stories.summary: Robeco's Thu Ha Chow sees Indonesia’s out-of-cycle interest rate hike this week as reflecting policymakers’ willingness to address investor concerns. Speaking on The China Show, Chow added that while Indonesia is at the beginning of a turnaround story, it’s not quite there yet. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: UK and European Stocks Set to Fall as Volatile Week Continues
Japan’s Shin-Etsu Plans New Rare-Earth Refinery to Secure Supply
stories.summary: Shin-Etsu Chemical Co., one of Japan’s largest rare-earth magnet makers, plans to build a new domestic refinery, the latest attempt by a company outside China to diversify its supply chain.

stories.headline: Albania’s Flamingo Revolution Spreads From Kushner Resort to Political Elite
stories.summary: As protests in Tirana extend into a second week, they have escalated from opposition to two €5 billion ($5.7 billion) planned resorts in ecologically sensitive areas linked to Jared Kushner into demands for the entire Albanian political establishment to step aside.

stories.headline: Asia’s Currency Fight Moves Offshore as Central Banks Push Back
stories.summary: Asian central banks are increasingly facing currency pressures originating outside their borders.

stories.headline: Hanuman Wind Pulls Bond Deal Again as Parent Governance Clouds
stories.summary: Hanuman Wind Power, a Hong Kong-based unit of troubled Thai renewable energy firm Energy Absolute Pcl, postponed a junk bond offering for a second time after it failed to attract the desired investor demand, according to a person familiar with the matter who asked not to be identified.

stories.headline: Federal Judge Blocks Alabama From Using Nitrogen Gas in Jeffrey Lee Execution
stories.summary: The judge ordered the state to find an alternate method if it is going to move ahead with the execution of Jeffery Lee, who was convicted of murder, and an appeals court upheld the decision.

stories.headline: Bolivian farmers clash with police as ex-president vows resistance
stories.summary: Bolivian farmers clash with police as ex-president vows resistance.

stories.summary: Protesters chant 'Albania is not for sale' as demonstrations swell against Kushner-backed luxury resort plan.

stories.headline: Anti-immigrant protesters clash with police, torch car at Belfast rally
stories.summary: Anti-immigrant demonstrators rallied for another day in Belfast, Northern Ireland, lighting a car on fire.

stories.headline: Nigerian migrants flee South Africa after spike in xenophobic protests
stories.summary: Migrants say they are living in fear after a group gave people living illegally in South Africa a deadline to leave.

stories.headline: Maasai women turn drought into income through fodder farming in Tanzania
stories.summary: As drought kills livestock, Maasai women are turning drought-resistant grass into animal feed and income.

stories.headline: Trump Officials Say ICE Won’t Raid World Cup Games, but Fans Are Worried
stories.summary: Federal agents are there for security, not immigration enforcement, officials say. But trust is fragile after the administration’s recent crackdowns.

stories.headline: Four days of extreme rain in Indonesia killed 7% of world’s rarest great apes, study finds
stories.summary: Critically endangered Tapanuli orangutan population falls after heavy rain and landslides, fuelled by climate crisis, in North SumatraExtreme rainfall and landslides fuelled by the climate crisis killed 7% of the remaining population of the world’s rarest great ape, a study has found, prompting fears for the species’ survival.The research suggests 58 out of the remaining 800 critically endangered Tapanuli orangutans (Pongo tapanuliensis) were kil

stories.headline: French star Patrick Bruel charged with rape and sexual assault
stories.summary: Singer and actor has denied all charges after more than 20 women came forward with allegations going back to the 1990sThe French singer Patrick Bruel has been charged with rape and sexual assault in one of the biggest #MeToo cases in the French music industry.The 67-year-old, a major figure in French pop culture, was placed under formal investigation over four cases that included alleged rape, attempted rape, sexual assault and sexual harassment.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 17, reports say
stories.summary: Nine of them were killed in a series of attacks in the town of Tayr Debba, according to Lebanon's state news agency.

stories.headline: French singer Patrick Bruel faces rape charges
stories.summary: Bruel has been placed under judicial investigation, which means an examining magistrate will look more deeply into the case.

stories.headline: Pakistan launches deadly air strikes in Afghanistan, reigniting tensions
stories.summary: The fresh strikes come after weeks of relative calm in the restive border region between the two countries.

stories.headline: Global Stocks Hit One-Month Low, Oil Gains on Iran: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: Global equities fell to a more than one-month low as a technology-led selloff deepened and the war in Iran showed little signs of ending.

stories.headline: Fear of the SaaSpocalypse is tormenting techland
stories.summary: Software once ate the world. Now it is in danger of eating itself

stories.headline: 'Partners and friends’: Trade and defence top of agenda at EU-South Korea summit
stories.summary: As security threats grow and geopolitics shift, the European Union and Republic of South Korea have celebrated a new Digital Trade Agreement at a summit in Brussels.

stories.headline: The vanishing of Nicolás Maduro: how the former dictator is being erased from Venezuela
stories.summary: Billboards are being painted over and former allies seem eager to forget the man they once glorifiedFor years, his bewhiskered face stared down from propaganda billboards glorifying the supposedly revolutionary rule of a dictator who styled himself as “the protector of the people”.The spin-doctored adoration was such that factories churned out plastic action figures exalting Nicolás Maduro as an “indestructible” and “iron-fisted” caped crusader n

stories.headline: Iran and Israel say they will pause strikes but warn of retaliation if ceasefire breached again
stories.summary: Israel's PM says his country is holding fire "at the moment", after Iran's armed forces said they had stopped military action.

stories.headline: Man reportedly shot at Kenya protest against US Ebola quarantine centre
stories.summary: Protesters are concerned about cross-border infection risks and the lack of transparency from the government about the treatment centre.

stories.headline: Two reportedly killed as women take part in rare protest in Afghanistan
stories.summary: The demonstration, in the western city of Herat, was broken up after police are alleged to have opened fire.

stories.headline: Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army
stories.summary: The BBC travels with rebels to frontline positions in Myanmar to see how the war is unfolding.

stories.headline: Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya
stories.summary: Police dispersed demonstrators in Nanyuki, 120 miles from Nairobi, amid rising anger at US plansKenyan police have shot dead a man during a protest against a proposed Ebola quarantine facility for US citizens.Patrick Wahome, who has organised protests in Nanyuki against the centre, told Reuters on Tuesday the man died from a gunshot wound to the head. Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound.

stories.headline: Murder of Lyhanna, 11, enrages France and turns up heat on government
stories.summary: Protesters are angry that the suspect had already been reported to police last August in a separate case.

stories.headline: World's largest chipmaker does not rule out price rises as costs increase
stories.summary: In a rare interview, a senior executive at TSMC discusses the AI boom, the geopolitics of chips and what it means for the price of electronics.

stories.headline: Former Air Canada pilot charged after allegedly flying without proper license for 16 years
stories.summary: Geoffrey Wall is alleged to have flown over 900 flights domestically and internationally between 2009 and 2025A former Air Canada pilot has been charged after flying for years without a proper license, Canadian police have said.Geoffrey Wall, of Barrie, Ontario, is alleged to have operated as an airline captain between 2009 and 2025 without a license to fly large commercial passenger planes, according to Peel regional police. stories.headline: Russia's fuel crisis intensifies as Ukraine steps up strikes on occupied territories
stories.summary: Kyiv has made it difficult for Moscow to provide military and civilian supplies to the territories it occupies.

stories.headline: Zelensky thanks UK for 'ironclad' support after meeting King
stories.summary: Following their meeting, Zelensky thanks the UK for its "ironclad" support for  Ukraine.

stories.headline: Couple arrested in Spain over gangland murder bid
stories.summary: The couple are alleged to have shot the victim four times on his doorstep in the San Javier district of Santiago de la Ribera in November 2024.

stories.headline: Armenia's pro-West government wins election despite Russian pressure
stories.summary: Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan's Civil Contract Party secures nearly 50% of the vote, comfortably beating the other contenders.

stories.headline: How one of India's most successful female politicians is losing her party
stories.summary: Mamata Banerjee's once-dominant Trinamool Congress party is unravelling, weeks after losing power in West Bengal.

stories.headline: Weather tracker: Monsoon season brings vital rainfall to parts of Asia
stories.summary: India declares onset as up to 280mm of rain falls in 72 hours in Kerala, while downpours hit south-west ThailandThe monsoon season has officially begun in parts of Asia, marking the start of a period of enhanced rainfall vital to the region’s economy.The south-west monsoon begins each year as a consequence of a growing temperature difference between the Asian land mass and the Indian Ocean. Through spring, the land heats up more rapidly than the

stories.headline: Zelensky's close European allies set out five conditions for peace talks
stories.summary: The Ukrainian president's meeting with his staunchest European allies came as US President Donald Trump's focus shifts to the war with Iran.

stories.headline: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
stories.summary: The Netherlands has one of the world's lowest rates of 16 to 24-year-olds not in education, employment or training.

stories.headline: Armenia votes as Russia piles pressure on pro-West government
stories.summary: Incumbent Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan is seeking a third term despite falling domestic support.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes in Gulf in latest test of ceasefire
stories.summary: The US military strikes Iranian drones and radar sites and Tehran says it has targeted US bases in Kuwait and Bahrain.

stories.headline: Funeral held for baby shot dead by Israeli troops in occupied West Bank
stories.summary: The seven-month-old boy's shrouded body was wrapped in a Palestinian flag and carried by his father to the graveyard.

stories.headline: Insecurity and instability drive voters in Peru's tight presidential race
stories.summary: After eight presidents in 10 years, many voters are looking for stability so the next president can focus on tackling crime and inequality.

stories.headline: Fifa bans all water bottles from World Cup games
stories.summary: Fifa has been warned it is putting fans' health at risk by introducing a bottle ban at this summer's World Cup.

stories.headline: Delcy Rodríguez visits India: Will oil talks lead to an energy deal?
stories.summary: Delcy Rodríguez's India visit highlights Venezuela's growing role in Delhi's oil diversification.

stories.headline: Protesters topple World Cup player statues in Mexico City
